description: Tier-1 compression fidelity on REAL transformer embeddings vs random Gaussian, to test whether the synthetic-data benchmark is representative.
finding: Real and random give identical ratio and cosine (within 0.001). The codec's fidelity is distribution-agnostic -- consistent with TurboQuant's random-rotation guarantee -- so the synthetic-data Tier-1 numbers are representative, neither optimistic nor pessimistic. This empirically answers the 'you used random data' critique (it does not matter), rather than the weaker 'real is even better' claim.
